article_title: Obligations for providers of general-purpose AI models
obligation_text: Providers of general-purpose AI models shall draw up and make publicly available a sufficiently detailed summary about the content used for training of the general-purpose AI model, according to a template provided by the AI Office.
scoring_text: Skill in creating AI training data transparency disclosures, including training content summarisation, compliance with AI Office template requirements, and public disclosure management.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in creating AI training data transparency disclosures, including training content summarisation, compliance with AI Office template requirements, and public disclosure management.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in designing data and AI training programs for diverse organizational audiences, from executives to operational staff, with role-appropriate content and delivery formats.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 53(1)(d) (obligation) superset of WIDAI OP domain. OP-S-002 is the strongest match (STS: 0.648, cosine: 0.671). Secondary: SP-T-010 (SP, cosine: 0.620), AG-K-007 (AG, cosine: 0.605).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.5/10 (raw 0.648). Shared vocabulary: [ai, content, data, skill, training]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [compliance, creating, disclosure, disclosures, management, office] vs WIDAI terms [appropriate, audiences, delivery, designing, diverse, executives] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.881 (P=0.884, R=0.877). Balanced P/R — the 22-word competency implication and 23-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (100.0%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the EU AI Act element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.671 ≈ STS 0.648 (gap 0.023). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [ai, content, data, skill, training] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 22.9%. Shared tokens: [ai, content, data, skill, training]. EU AI Act-only: [compliance, creating, disclosure, disclosures, management, office]. WIDAI-only: [appropriate, audiences, delivery, designing, diverse, executives].
